source: EVAR planner Set-A annotation SOP (docs/SEGMENTATION_ANNOTATION_SOP.md). The scheme the solo annotator paints in 3D Slicer for own-institution pre-op CTAs.
notes: Set A = lumen + branches (generalization fix, GOAL #41). NOT wall/ILT (that is Set B, GOAL #37). Paint IDs 4-13 deliberately equal the AortaSeg24 raw ids for the abdominal branches so our own annotations and the public dataset share one label space (roadmap principle 5); id 1 (abdominal aorta lumen, painted as ONE label rather than AortaSeg24's zones 19-23) and ids 24/25 (common femorals, which AortaSeg24 lacks) are additions. pipeline_label maps into the app scheme 1=aorta 2/3=iliacs 4/5=CFAs 6/7=renals 8=celiac 9=SMA. Internal iliacs (12/13) are painted for completeness but map to 0 until the pipeline gains labels 12/13. BEFORE training, load one finished mask through autoseg.aortaseg24.translate_labels with THIS map and eyeball it in the GUI to confirm the ids round-trip.

classes.name: abdominal_aorta_lumen
classes.comment: diaphragmatic hiatus/celiac-2cm down to the aortic bifurcation; contrast lumen only, excludes wall + thrombus

classes.name: celiac_artery
classes.comment: proximal trunk ~2-3 cm from origin only

classes.name: superior_mesenteric_artery
classes.comment: proximal trunk only; SMA origin is the lower infrarenal-neck reference

classes.name: left_renal_artery
classes.comment: ostium + proximal ~1-2 cm, stop before hilar branching

classes.name: left_external_iliac
classes.comment: internal iliac origin -> inferior epigastric a. (inguinal ligament); merges to pipeline iliac

classes.name: right_external_iliac
classes.comment: internal iliac origin -> inferior epigastric a.

classes.name: left_internal_iliac
classes.comment: painted to first division only; not yet in pipeline scheme (could become 12)

classes.name: right_internal_iliac
classes.comment: not yet in pipeline scheme (could become 13)

classes.name: left_common_femoral
classes.comment: inferior epigastric a. -> femoral bifurcation (SFA/profunda); the distal EVAR access/seal landmark
